title: Email Marketing Software Comparison for 2026
description: A detailed comparison of the leading email marketing platforms including Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign, and HubSpot across features, pricing, and automation capabilities.
body:
Email marketing platforms vary significantly in their automation depth, contact management capabilities, and pricing models. Choosing the right platform requires matching your email program's complexity with the platform's strengths. This comparison evaluates the leading email marketing platforms across the dimensions that matter most for different types of senders.

## HubSpot Marketing Hub

HubSpot Marketing Hub offers email marketing as part of its broader marketing platform, integrating email campaigns with CRM, content management, and analytics. Its free tier includes core email and contact management features for up to 1 million contacts. The Starter tier at $15 per month for up to 1,000 contacts adds basic automation. The Professional tier at $800 per month for up to 2,000 contacts includes sequences, custom reporting, and team management. The Enterprise tier at $3,600 per month for up to 10,000 contacts adds predictive lead scoring and custom objects.

HubSpot is best suited for organizations that want a unified marketing, sales, and service platform with integrated email capabilities. Its strength is cross-functional data flow rather than email-only features.

## Mailchimp

Mailchimp is one of the most widely recognized email marketing platforms, offering a free tier for up to 2,000 contacts with core email and automation features. Its paid tiers add advanced segmentation, multivariate testing, and custom-coded templates. Mailchimp's strength is its ease of use and extensive template library, making it a popular choice for small to mid-sized businesses. Current pricing for each tier is available on Mailchimp's pricing page.

## ActiveCampaign

ActiveCampaign specializes in marketing automation with deep email sequencing, conditional content, and lead scoring capabilities. Its platform is designed for marketers who need sophisticated automation workflows beyond basic broadcast email. ActiveCampaign's pricing is tiered by contact count and feature access, with current rates published on its pricing page.

## Choosing the Right Platform

Small businesses and solopreneurs with basic email needs should evaluate Mailchimp's free tier or HubSpot's free CRM with email capabilities. Marketers who need sophisticated automation workflows will find ActiveCampaign's sequencing and lead scoring capabilities strongest. Organizations that want email marketing integrated with a broader CRM and sales platform should choose HubSpot for its unified data model. Consider your contact list size, automation complexity requirements, and integration needs when making a decision. Most platforms offer free trials, so testing with your actual workflows is recommended.
author: PilotStack Team
